channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Too Big for Shangri-La | Foreign Policy
item.description: Snubbing a key security forum shows Beijing’s new confidence.

item.title: 🌏 New DPRK-Russia Friendship Hospital is Repurposing Existing Project | 38 North
item.description: In late April, when officials from Russia and North Korea broke ground on the new DPRK-Russia ... The post New DPRK-Russia Friendship Hospital is Repurposing Existing Project appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 📍 Brazil plans to buy 20 more Gripen fighters | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Marcelo Kanitz Damasceno, the Brazilian air chief, told Breaking Defense that the need for extra jets was born from a serious look at Brazil’s air power.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s New ‘Forced Labor’ Tariffs Are a Fig Leaf | Foreign Policy
item.description: The new import duties are “a solution in search of a problem.”

item.title: 🌏 Is Time on China’s Side? Beijing’s Taiwan Calculus and the Balance of Power | War on the Rocks
item.description: When is the risk of war the highest? And what should the United States be doing about it? One of the most important but underappreciated questions in international politics is how states think about the future balance of power. Countries that believe their position is improving…

item.title: 📍 A US oil export ban could raise pump prices | Atlantic Council
item.description: If the Strait of Hormuz closure continues and gasoline prices increase further, US policymakers might consider banning oil exports. But instead of lowering prices, it could have the opposite effect. The post A US oil export ban could raise pump prices appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon’s Cyber Defense Command drafting plan to defend critical infrastructure | Breaking Defense
item.description: DoD Cyber Defense Command is working to develop a joint task force for defense of critical infrastructure.

item.title: 🌍 Hidden chemical weapons sites emerge in Syria amid fragile security transition | Defense News
item.description: Over a decade after Syria agreed to dismantle its arsenal, international inspectors have uncovered scores of previously hidden chemical weapons materials.

item.title: 🏛️ A Navy carrier is about to deploy with a robot ship. Could it change the service forever? | Breaking Defense
item.description: Experts said that this deployment could lay the foundation for how the Navy develops its concept of operations for unmanned systems.

item.title: 📍 Project Anthracite: Sunchon Area Site Profile | 38 North
item.description: This research draws on Project Anthracite, a multiyear initiative funded by Global Affairs ... The post Project Anthracite: Sunchon Area Site Profile appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🌍 Iraq’s militias are fracturing as Iran turns from theocracy to juntocracy | Atlantic Council
item.description: Iraq’s militias face a spiritual and organizational dilemma that gravely endangers their existence, in the wake of Iran's leadership change. The post Iraq’s militias are fracturing as Iran turns from theocracy to juntocracy appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 How to boost defense aviation in an election year? Sen. Budd has a few ideas. | Breaking Defense
item.description: North Carolina Sen. Ted Budd outlines his concerns for defense firms as lawmakers get closer to the midterm elections.

item.title: 🏴 Dutch plant for combat-zone robots offers fresh supply pipeline for Ukraine | Defense News
item.description: The facility has already produced its first THeMIS vehicle for the Dutch government, part of a run of more than 100 such robots pledged by the Netherlands.

item.title: 🌍 Sanctions Gaps and the Governance of Corruption Risk | Just Security
item.description: U.S. foreign policy expert examines how overlapping U.N., U.S., and EU sanctions regimes create legal gray zones and why that breeds corruption risk. The post Sanctions Gaps and the Governance of Corruption Risk appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Thales ramps up guided rocket production to address increased ME demand | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We more than doubled the [production] number, considering the situation at the moment in the Middle East,” a company executive told Breaking Defense.

item.title: 🌏 China’s Global ‘Concierge Services’ to Strengthen Fellow Authoritarians | Just Security
item.description: China's intrusive military, economic, and diplomatic aid to Russia, Iran, and others spreads autocratic practices such as secrecy, censorship, surveillance, and corruption. The post China’s Global ‘Concierge Services’ to Strengthen Fellow Authoritarians appeared first on Just…

item.title: 📍 Are We Reliving the 1890s, 1930s, or 1950s? | Foreign Policy
item.description: What history can and cannot explain about today’s unraveling order.

item.title: 📍 Bang, Bang, Bang: Callais Kills Off the Voting Rights Act | Just Security
item.description: To the extent that the Voting Rights Act served as at least a minimal constraint on political gerrymandering, that constraint is gone. The post Bang, Bang, Bang: Callais Kills Off the Voting Rights Act appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Tracing Digital Links Between Viory and Ruptly | Bellingcat
item.description: “In the age of misinformation, the line between fact and fiction is blurrier than ever.” “For those of us working in video news, verification isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s a necessity. It is how we protect the stories we help shape and how we earn and maintain trust in an…

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 4, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump privately told aides he would consider ending the ceasefire with Iran if Tehran kills…

item.title: 🏴 Estonia Has Lessons for Us All | Foreign Policy
item.description: The small Baltic country has refashioned the relationship between citizens and the state for the current moment.

item.title: 📍 Rheinmetall sells struggling auto division to focus on defense | Defense News
item.description: The unit will be sold to Aequita for a provisional €350 million, subject to potential adjustment when the deal closes, expected in the fourth quarter.

item.title: 🌍 The Intersection of Sanctions and Corruption Symposium | Just Security
item.description: Just Security and Perry World House bring together experts to examine how sanctions and anti-corruption policy interact and how to make accountability tools more effective. The post The Intersection of Sanctions and Corruption Symposium appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏴 Vladimir Putin’s Second-Biggest Headache | Foreign Policy
item.description: With his war in Ukraine going badly, he may soon face another quagmire in Chechnya.

item.title: 📍 The promise and limits of the new G20 template for debt restructuring | Atlantic Council
item.description: A new G20 roadmap aims to streamline sovereign debt restructurings. Yet by reducing flexibility for creditors and debtors alike, it could make future debt crises harder to resolve. The post The promise and limits of the new G20 template for debt restructuring appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 Forged in a Knife Fight: China’s Brutal Domestic AI Competition | War on the Rocks
item.description: China’s plan to become a world leader in AI by 2030 is a fixture of practically every Congressional briefing and expert commentary on Beijing’s AI ambitions. The plan’s logic — introduced in 2017 — was simple and alarming: Beijing would direct capital, mobilize its firms,…

item.title: 🌏 Revisiting The Importance of the Battle of Midway | War on the Rocks
item.description: The Battle of Midway has assumed a place in American naval lore that has put it on par with other great battles in world naval history. What Salamis was for the Greeks, Trafalgar for the British Royal Navy, and Tsushima for the Japanese, the clash northwest of Midway Island on…

item.title: 🌍 Wrong Audience, Wrong Ask: Why Trump’s Abraham Accords Gambit Falls on Deaf Ears | War on the Rocks
item.description: When President Donald Trump repeatedly pressed regional leaders on Abraham Accords expansion late last month — framing Arab-Israeli normalization as a debt owed and a condition for a settlement to end the Iran war — he apparently commented there had been silence on the other end…

item.title: 🌏 The missile gap problem: US Indo-Pacific deterrence under strain | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The US commitment to Indo-Pacific stability is being tested not by strategic ambiguity but by industrial reality. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth’s recent notification to Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi that deliveries of US-made Tomahawk ...

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Mafia State Understands Only Law Backed by Power | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The National Interest: Iran’s Mafia State Understands Only Law Backed by Power appeared first on Atlantic Council.
